# Implementation Plan: Clipboard Image Auto Capture

**Implementation Branch**: `main` (feature label: `006-clipboard-image-capture`) | **Date**: 2026-06-28 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/006-clipboard-image-capture/spec.md`

**Note**: Generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow. This plan adds local, Apple-native image clipboard capture to the existing automatic capture pipeline while preserving text capture, local-first storage, privacy-by-default behavior, row actions, and design-system consistency.

## Summary

Extend the existing clipboard monitor so one polling path handles text and image clipboard payloads. Image payloads are captured first when the system clipboard exposes Apple-decodable raster image data; screenshots and copied images share this path. Valid image data up to 25 MiB (26,214,400 bytes) encoded size is decoded for validation and duplicate identity, stored full-resolution in app-private local file storage without recompression, represented in SwiftData by metadata/hash/local file references only, and displayed in history with capture-time generated local thumbnails. Image clips use `contentType = "image"`, support copy/delete/pin, copy back the preserved full image payload, and never transmit clipboard content off-device.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Swift in the existing Xcode project. Checked-in project settings currently use `SWIFT_VERSION = 5.0` with current Xcode Apple SDKs.

**Primary Dependencies**: SwiftUI, SwiftData, Foundation, AppKit on macOS for `NSPasteboard` image read/write, UIKit pasteboard support where Apple-platform targets expose it, UniformTypeIdentifiers/ImageIO/CoreGraphics for Apple-native image type detection, decoding, dimensions, and thumbnail generation, and CryptoKit or an equivalent Apple-native digest API for duplicate hashes. No third-party image libraries, telemetry, OCR, AI, or CloudKit sync.

**Storage**: SwiftData remains the local history source of truth for clip records. Full-resolution image payloads and generated thumbnails are stored as files under app-private Application Support storage; SwiftData stores only `contentType`, timestamps, pin state, duplicate hash, dimensions, byte count, original type identifier/extension, local full-image reference, and local thumbnail reference.

**Testing**: Swift Testing in `NextPasteTests` for image payload validation, PNG/JPEG/screenshot fixture detection, oversize/corrupt/unsupported rejection, normalized pixel-hash deduplication, local file persistence/cleanup, thumbnail generation, image copy-back success/failure, and text capture regressions. XCTest UI automation in `NextPasteUITests` for automatic image history refresh, thumbnail visibility, active/backgrounded/minimized capture, image copy/delete/pin, text auto-capture regression, and row-action regression.

**Target Platform**: Existing multi-platform Apple app (`iphoneos`, `iphonesimulator`, `macosx`, `xros`, `xrsimulator`) with macOS as the current executable UI-test platform. Platform clipboard differences stay behind compile-time adapters; macOS tests cover NSPasteboard behavior.

**Project Type**: Single Xcode SwiftUI app with one app target, one Swift Testing unit target, and one XCTest UI test target.

**Performance Goals**: Clipboard polling remains lightweight and bounded by the existing configurable interval. Capture handles only one changed clipboard payload per observed change count, rejects encoded image payloads over 25 MiB (26,214,400 bytes) before persistence, generates one bounded thumbnail during capture, and avoids full-image recompression.

**Constraints**: Preserve existing text capture behavior and text row actions. Do not add OCR, AI analysis, CloudKit sync, third-party image libraries, analytics, remote transmission, manual image import, share extensions, shortcuts, startup login items, or image editing. Use Apple-native APIs only. UI changes must reuse the design system. SonarQube Project Health evidence is mandatory after implementation.

**Scale/Scope**: Extend the existing automatic history list and row-action architecture for image clips. Add focused app files under `NextPaste/` for image payloads, storage, hashing, thumbnail generation, and image copy-back. Add focused unit/UI test files under `NextPasteTests/` and `NextPasteUITests/`. Avoid unrelated feature work.

## Implementation Architecture

### Capture Pipeline

The existing `ClipboardMonitor` continues to observe pasteboard `changeCount` while the process is alive. Replace the current text-only read with a payload snapshot from `ClipboardPasteboardReader`:

1. `ClipboardMonitor` detects a changed pasteboard count.
2. `ClipboardPasteboardReader` returns a single prioritized `ClipboardPayload`: image first when usable image candidates exist, otherwise text.
3. `ClipboardCaptureService` routes the payload through `captureClipboardPayload(_:observedAt:)` while keeping a compatibility text entry point for existing tests/manual save code.
4. For image payloads, validation rejects missing data, unsupported type identifiers, decode failures, zero-sized images, and encoded payloads over 25 MiB (26,214,400 bytes).
5. Validation decodes the image with Apple-native APIs to stable pixel data and dimensions, then computes a duplicate identity from normalized decoded pixels plus width and height.
6. If a SwiftData image clip with the same duplicate identity already exists, capture returns duplicate and does not write files.
7. A local full-image file is written using the selected encoded pasteboard representation exactly as received, without recompression.
8. A local thumbnail is generated during capture from decoded image data and stored separately for row display.
9. A `ClipItem` with `contentType = "image"`, image metadata, local references, and default unpinned state is inserted and saved.
10. SwiftData `@Query` refreshes the existing history list automatically.

Text-only payloads continue through the existing validation, text deduplication, persistence, and history refresh path. If a clipboard item includes usable image data plus textual metadata, the image path wins and no metadata-only text clip is created. If an image candidate is present but invalid, unsupported, corrupted, inaccessible, empty, or oversized, no clip is created for that change.

### Storage Architecture

Add an `ImageClipFileStore` responsible for app-private local file URLs and atomic-ish writes:

- Full images: `Application Support/NextPaste/Clips/Images/<clip-id>.<source-extension>`
- Thumbnails: `Application Support/NextPaste/Clips/Thumbnails/<clip-id>.png`
- UI tests/unit tests inject a temporary root so tests never touch a real user store.
- File writes occur before SwiftData insertion; if SwiftData save fails, the service removes any files written for that failed capture.
- Delete of an image clip removes both local files after the SwiftData delete succeeds, or records/report errors consistently with existing app error handling if file cleanup fails.
- Copy-back reads the preserved full image file and writes it to the system clipboard with the stored type identifier.

SwiftData remains the queryable history source. The file store only holds binary payloads and thumbnails and does not transmit, sync, or analyze content.

### Data Model Changes

Extend `ClipItem` rather than introducing a separate history model so text and image clips continue to sort, pin, delete, and display through one `@Query`:

- Existing fields remain: `id`, `contentType`, `textContent`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `isPinned`, `pinnedSortOrder`.
- Add optional image metadata fields: `imageHash`, `imageWidth`, `imageHeight`, `imageByteCount`, `imageUTType`, `imageFilename`, `thumbnailFilename`, and `thumbnailDescription`.
- Text clips keep `contentType = "text"` and nil image fields.
- Image clips use `contentType = "image"`, empty or non-displayed text fallback content, and populated image fields.

Migration is expected to be a lightweight SwiftData migration because new fields are optional or have safe defaults. Existing text clips must remain readable and keep unchanged text, ordering, pin state, copy/delete/pin behavior, and content type.

### Thumbnail Generation and UI

Add an Apple-native `ImageThumbnailGenerator` that decodes the validated full image data and writes a thumbnail during capture. The thumbnail is derived display data only; it does not replace or recompress the preserved full image payload.

`ClipRowView` selects the row presentation by `contentType`:

- Text clips continue through `ClipboardRow` and `ClipboardRowPresentation`.
- Image clips use `ImageClipboardRow` with a local thumbnail image, aspect-fit scaling, no cropping, fixed design-token-sized thumbnail area, and existing card/badge/action styling.
- The fallback icon using `DesignTokens.Icons.image` appears only when a valid image clip exists but thumbnail loading/generation failed.
- Accessibility identifiers distinguish image rows and thumbnail surfaces while preserving row action identifiers (`copy-clip-button`, `delete-clip-button`, `pin-clip-button`) for shared UI tests.

### Copy/Delete/Pin Behavior

`HomeView.copyClip(_:)` delegates by content type:

- Text clips continue using `ClipboardWriter.copy(_ text:)` with existing success/failure feedback behavior.
- Image clips call an image copy-back path that reads the preserved full image file and writes that data to the system clipboard.
- Image copy failure leaves the clipboard unchanged and clears/does not show copied feedback, matching existing text failure behavior.
- Delete removes SwiftData history entries and associated image/thumbnail files.
- Pin toggles the existing `isPinned`/`pinnedSortOrder` values for both text and image clips.

### Error Handling

Validation failures are explicit capture outcomes (`unsupportedImage`, `emptyImage`, `oversizedImage`, `decodeFailed`, `duplicate`) rather than broad silent failures. Persistence failures roll back SwiftData and clean up written files. Copy-back failures do not modify the clipboard and do not show success-shaped UI feedback.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Pre-Research Gate

- **Clipboard-first product**: PASS. The feature is triggered by system clipboard changes and explicitly preserves `Clipboard Changed -> Detect -> Validate -> Deduplicate -> Persist -> Refresh UI`.
- **Local-first architecture**: PASS. SwiftData metadata plus app-private files are local sources of truth; image capture, persistence, history display, and row actions work offline.
- **Privacy by default**: PASS. Image data stays on-device. The plan adds no telemetry, analytics, Firebase, advertising SDKs, remote APIs, CloudKit sync, OCR, or AI analysis.
- **Automatic capture**: PASS. The plan defines content-type identification, image validation, image/text duplicate handling, local persistence, and automatic SwiftData history refresh.
- **Test-first coverage**: PASS. Unit and UI test coverage is planned for image detection, deduplication, unsupported/corrupt/oversized rejection, local persistence, thumbnails, row actions, text regressions, offline behavior, and Sonar evidence.
- **Native simplicity**: PASS. The plan uses Apple-native platform APIs and existing app structure only; no third-party image dependency is introduced. Crypto/digest and image-decoding APIs are Apple-native system frameworks needed for robust duplicate identity and image handling.
- **SonarQube project health gate**: PASS. Post-implementation SonarQube evidence is required before the feature is complete.
- **Consistent design system**: PASS. Image rows reuse existing design tokens, row cards, badges, icons, spacing, radius, and accessibility conventions.
- **Refactoring integrity**: PASS. Existing text capture and row actions are preserved with regression coverage; no speculative architecture beyond the image payload/storage seams required by this feature.

### Post-Design Gate

- **Clipboard-first product**: PASS. Design artifacts specify one automatic image/text capture pipeline and history refresh via SwiftData.
- **Local-first architecture**: PASS. Data model and contracts keep binary image data in app-private files and metadata in SwiftData; no network or sync dependency exists.
- **Privacy by default**: PASS. Contracts prohibit remote transmission and third-party analytics; quickstart includes offline/local-first validation.
- **Automatic capture**: PASS. Contracts define active/backgrounded/minimized detection, validation, deduplication, persistence, and UI refresh outcomes.
- **Test-first coverage**: PASS. Quickstart lists targeted Swift Testing and XCUITest commands before full-suite validation.
- **Native simplicity**: PASS. Design uses existing Xcode targets and Apple frameworks with compile-time platform adapters.
- **SonarQube project health gate**: PASS. Quickstart requires recorded SonarQube evidence or documented false-positive evidence before completion.
- **Consistent design system**: PASS. UI contract requires aspect-fit thumbnails in tokenized row surfaces and fallback icon only on thumbnail failure.
- **Refactoring integrity**: PASS. Existing text behavior and row actions are required regression checks.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/006-clipboard-image-capture/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   ├── capture-pipeline-contract.md
│   ├── history-ui-contract.md
│   └── storage-contract.md
└── tasks.md              # Created later by /speckit.tasks
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
NextPaste/
├── ClipItem.swift                         # Extend SwiftData metadata for image clips
├── ClipboardCaptureService.swift          # Route text/image payload capture
├── ClipboardMonitor.swift                 # Poll payload snapshots instead of text only
├── ClipboardMonitorClient.swift           # Pasteboard adapter for text/image payloads
├── ClipboardWriter.swift                  # Add image copy-back path
├── ClipRowView.swift                      # Select text vs image row presentation
├── HomeView.swift                         # Route row rendering/actions by content type
├── DesignSystem/Components/
│   ├── ClipboardRowPresentation.swift     # Preserve text presentation, extend image metadata
│   └── ImageClipboardRow.swift            # Display local thumbnail/fallback icon
└── ImageClips/
    ├── ClipboardImagePayload.swift        # Validated image payload value
    ├── ImageClipFileStore.swift           # App-private full image/thumbnail file storage
    ├── ImageDuplicateIdentity.swift       # Normalized decoded pixel hash + dimensions
    └── ImageThumbnailGenerator.swift      # Capture-time local thumbnail generation

NextPasteTests/
├── ClipboardImageCaptureTests.swift       # Detection, validation, dedupe, persistence
├── ImageClipFileStoreTests.swift          # Local storage and cleanup behavior
├── ImageThumbnailGeneratorTests.swift     # Thumbnail generation/fallback inputs
├── ClipboardWriterTests.swift             # Text/image copy success/failure behavior
├── ClipboardCaptureTests.swift            # Existing text monitor regression additions
└── SwiftDataTestSupport.swift             # Temporary image store/test fixture helpers

NextPasteUITests/
├── ClipboardImageAutoCaptureUITests.swift # End-to-end image capture/thumbnail/row actions
├── ClipboardRobot.swift                   # Add PNG/JPEG/screenshot pasteboard fixtures
├── RowRobot.swift                         # Reuse image row action targeting
├── UITestAssertions.swift                 # Add image thumbnail/row assertions
└── UITestFixtures.swift                   # Add deterministic image fixture metadata
```

**Structure Decision**: Keep one Xcode app and the existing file-system-synchronized groups. Extend `ClipItem` for mixed text/image history rather than adding a parallel history model. Add small focused image helper files under `NextPaste/ImageClips/` to keep binary storage, hashing, and thumbnail behavior testable without changing the broader SwiftData flow.

## Phase 0 Research Summary

Research decisions are recorded in [research.md](research.md). All technical context questions are resolved by the clarified specification and current code inspection: Apple-decodable raster image payloads, one shared screenshot/copied-image path, app-private file storage for binaries, SwiftData metadata only, normalized decoded-pixel deduplication, capture-time thumbnails, copy-back from preserved full image data, and mandatory text behavior regression coverage.

## Phase 1 Design Summary

The data model is recorded in [data-model.md](data-model.md). Capture, storage, and UI contracts are recorded under [contracts/](contracts/). Validation and run guidance, including unit/UI test commands and Sonar evidence requirements, are recorded in [quickstart.md](quickstart.md). The managed agent-context plan reference points to this feature plan.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations are present. No complexity exceptions are required.
